panels.visual_description: Watercolor style. Extreme Close-up on Professor Aronnax's forehead. Beads of cold perspiration are rendered as watery, blue ink bleeds running down his skin. The background is a wash of murky, anxious teals and greys, representing his internal fear. Soft, diffused lighting.
panels.dialogue: Going up and down at the bottom of the sea... where one is almost certain to meet sharks...

panels.visual_description: Medium shot. The door to Aronnax's cabin slides open. Ned Land and Conseil enter, framed by a warm, inviting yellow light that bleeds softly into the gloom of the room. They look composed and joyous, contrasting with the previous panel's tension.
panels.dialogue: Faith, sir! Your Captain Nemo—the devil take him!—has just made us a very pleasant offer.

panels.visual_description: Two-shot. Aronnax looks up, his face pale with a wash of white watercolor, while Conseil leans in eagerly. The background suggests the rivets of the Nautilus walls with loose, wet brushstrokes.
panels.dialogue: If agreeable to you, sir, the commander has invited us to visit the magnificent Ceylon fisheries to-morrow.

panels.visual_description: Wide shot. The three men sit on a plush ottoman. The textures of the velvet fabric are rendered in deep reds and violets with soft edges. The atmosphere is academic but cozy. Aronnax is gesturing like a teacher.
panels.dialogue: Sit down, my friends. To the poet, a pearl is a tear of the sea; to the Orientals, a drop of solidified dew.

panels.visual_description: Abstract watercolor montage. A thought bubble visualization blending with the scene: a glowing, opalescent pearl forming inside a loose, ink-wash oyster shell. The colors are iridescent pinks, creams, and silvers bleeding into the surrounding water.
panels.dialogue: For the naturalist, it is simply a morbid secretion... a nacreous formation deposited around a grain of sand, year after year.

panels.visual_description: Close-up on Aronnax. His eyes are wide, staring into the middle distance, the anxiety returning. The color palette shifts slightly back to the cold blues of the first page as his mind wanders to his fears.
panels.dialogue: One oyster has been mentioned... though I doubt it... as having contained no less than a hundred and fifty sharks.

panels.visual_description: Reaction shot. Ned Land looks baffled, eyebrows raised. The background is a stark, dry-brush white to emphasize the sudden confusion.
Medium shot. Aronnax waves his hands hurriedly, a flush of embarrassed red watercolor spreading on his cheeks. Conseil looks on with a small, knowing smile.
panels.dialogue: Did I say sharks? I meant pearls! Sharks would not be sense.

panels.visual_description: Low angle, looking up at Aronnax. He has recovered his composure. The lighting is melancholic, casting long, soft shadows. He explains the process with a somber gravity.
panels.dialogue: The most common way is to lay the oysters on mats of seaweed. They die in the open air, and at the end of ten days, they are in a forward state of decomposition.

panels.visual_description: Macro shot of a 'Virgin Pearl' or 'Paragon'. It floats in a void of deep indigo watercolor. It is perfectly round, glowing with an inner light, white and opaque like a moon in a night sky.
panels.dialogue: The most beautiful are called virgin pearls, or paragons. Formed alone in the tissue of the mollusc... white, often opaque.

panels.visual_description: Group shot, pulling back. The three figures are small in the frame, surrounded by the vast, ornate machinery of the Nautilus interior. The metalwork is rendered in sepia and brass tones, suggesting the enormity of the vessel and the ocean outside.
panels.dialogue: They have the transparency of an opal; they are generally round or oval. These are the treasures we seek.
